     Phase 0 of docs/agents/visual-quality-review.goal.md. 20 confirmed / 13 rejected, 39 agents. -->

# Phase-0 Visual-Defect Catalog — viney.ca

## 1. Executive Summary

This catalog confirms **20 defects** (0 P0, 6 P1, 7 P2, 7 P3; severities S2–S4). They cluster into four themes: (a) **responsive layout failures** — a nested `max-width` strangulation where `.main-content{max-width:900px}` clamps every `.topic-page`/`.econ-topic-page`/`.economist-article` wrapper that lacks a `:has()` escape, plus fixed-width chart images and dashboard tables that overflow on phones; (b) **content/excerpt quality** — auto-excerpts that lead with "Source:" citations, duplicate/conflicting `<head>` metadata on every page, and all-caps full-sentence hero captions; (c) **cross-page consistency** — three divergent post-card implementations, category cards that bypass the WebP `responsive-image` include, layout-less `/agents/*` pages, and inconsistent CTA/active-state contracts; and (d) a meta **coverage-gap cluster** that explains all the rest. The headline reason the gate missed everything: **there is no merge-blocking visual gate at all.** BackstopJS — the only pixel-diff tool — runs `continue-on-error: true` (and uploads its report only `if: failure()`), the blocking Playwright suite has **zero** `toHaveScreenshot` assertions and its `@visual` layout tests were "nuclear-healed" to accept any positioning, and the scenario/URL list never enumerates category, `/agents/*`, dashboard, `/search/`, or `/404/` pages. Existing defects were therefore baked into baselines as "expected" rather than flagged.

## 2. Prioritized Defect Table

| Priority | Severity | Type | Location | What the reader sees | Recommended guard |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| P1 | S2 | responsive | `/security/`, `/software-engineering/`, `/test-automation/`; `_sass/economist-theme.scss` `.topic-page`/`.main-content` | 3-column card grid crushed into a centered ~556px column (153px cards, titles wrapping 3–4 lines, shrunken images) with ~442px empty gutters each side at every desktop width >1024px | BackstopJS desktop scenario for a category page + Playwright assert first `.topic-card` width ≥ ~280px; add `.main-content:has(.topic-page)` width escape and drop the hardcoded 148px padding |
| P1 | S2 | content | `/blog/`, `/`, all category pages, in-post related/more-from rails; root cause `_posts/2026-04-05-cost-of-poor-quality-copq.md:15-16` (+21 more) | ~half of all cards open with "Source: GitClear…/OpenText…" citation boilerplate instead of an article lede; teasers look broken | Assert no teaser selector text matches `/^\s*Source:/i` across `/`, `/blog/`, category pages; build-time lint in `validate-post-quality.sh` rejecting image+`*Source:*` opener with no prose before excerpt break |
| P1 | S2 | content | all pages; `_layouts/default.html:8,9,11` + `{% seo %}` line 25 | Two conflicting `<title>`, `<meta description>`, and canonical per page (browsers show first tab title, but crawlers/social pick either) | Extend `seo-jsonld.spec.ts` to assert (without `.first()`) `toHaveCount(1)` for `head title`, `link[rel=canonical]`, `meta[name=description]`; remove manual lines 8/9/11 and let `{% seo %}` own them |
| P1 | S2 | consistency | `/agents/code-reviewer/`, `/agents/security-auditor/`, `/agents/test-engineer/`; `agents/*.md` (no `layout:`) | Raw unstyled black-on-white text, no doctype/head/nav/footer; published and in `sitemap.xml`, leaks internal agent-prompt definitions | CI invariant walking every `_site/**/index.html` (or `sitemap.xml`) asserting doctype/`<html>`/site-chrome; front-matter validator requiring `layout:`; immediate fix: exclude `agents/*.md` from build |
| P1 | S2 | coverage-gap | `.github/workflows/test-quality.yml:142-144` | Any pixel regression (broken layout, shifted spacing, wrong color) on covered pages can ship unblocked; diff report often not even uploaded | Remove `continue-on-error: true` from the visual-regression step and make it a required check; change report upload to `if: always()` |
| P1 | S2 | coverage-gap | `tests/playwright-agents/*.spec.ts` | A CSS/rendering regression (color, drop cap, spacing, overlap, font swap) can ship undetected — no blocking check compares pixels | Commit BackstopJS reference bitmaps for the 5×3 scenarios and make blocking; or add `toHaveScreenshot` baselines for core page types across Mobile/Tablet/Desktop in a non-`continue-on-error` job |
| P2 | S2 | responsive | `/2026/01/19/the-surprising-economics-of-test-automation-roi/`, `/2026/04/12/platform-engineering-adoption-crisis/`, `/2026/04/12/the-hidden-economics-of-security-debt/`; `_sass/economist-theme.scss:595` | On any phone <~520px the inline chart renders at fixed 500px: whole page gains horizontal scroll and the chart is clipped off the right | Playwright at 320/390px asserting `scrollWidth <= clientWidth+1` and chart img width ≤ viewport; restore `responsive.spec.ts:467`; CSS fix `max-width: min(500px, 100%)` |
| P2 | S2 | consistency | `/security/`, `/software-engineering/`, `/test-automation/`; `security-topic.md:21` etc. | Category thumbnails served as multi-MB PNGs (~5.8MB on /security/) vs ~0.6MB WebP everywhere else — noticeably slower, worst on mobile | Playwright/HTML lint failing on raw `<img>` whose `.png` src has a `.webp` sibling; Lighthouse "next-gen formats" budget on /security/; route images through `responsive-image.html` |
| P2 | S3 | content | all posts with `image_caption`; `_layouts/post.html:44` (`\| upcase`) | Every hero caption is a 60–80 char letter-spaced ALL-CAPS sentence, hard to read, wrapping over multiple lines on mobile | `validate-post-quality.sh` rule flagging `image_caption` over a credit-length budget (~40 chars); Playwright assert `figcaption.image-credit` length within budget |
| P2 | S3 | consistency | `/blog/`, `/security/` + 2, `/` ; `blog/index.html`, `security-topic.md`, `index.md` | Same articles render as visibly different cards across pages — 3:2 vs 16:9 crop, red vs grey category label, top-border vs white flex framing | Cross-page Playwright spec asserting post cards resolve to one canonical selector across all listing pages; extract single `_includes/post-card.html` |
| P2 | S2 | coverage-gap | `/security/`, `/software-engineering/`, `/test-automation/` | No defect today; risk that a broken topic-grid/header on a primary nav destination ships undetected at any viewport | Add the 3 category URLs to `backstop.json` at all 3 viewports; parametrized `responsive.spec.ts` asserting `.topic-grid` visible + no overflow at 320/768/1920 |
| P2 | S2 | coverage-gap | all pages, 320px (`responsive.spec.ts:723-724`, `:467-511`) | `/dashboard/agents.html` currently scrolls horizontally at 320px (DIV.chart-card forced to 340px, no `min-width:0`); any future wide table/code/URL would ship the same | Playwright at 320px iterating every page type asserting `scrollWidth <= clientWidth+1` and naming the offending node; add `min-width:0` to `.chart-card` |
| P2 | S3 | coverage-gap | `tests/playwright-agents/responsive.spec.ts` (Blog grid) | Nothing today; latent — a regression collapsing the desktop 3-col grid or failing mobile stacking would pass (tests accept `x>=0,y>=0,width>20`) | Replace vacuous coordinate checks with breakpoint assertions on `gridTemplateColumns` track count (3/2/1 at 1920/768/320); promote BackstopJS Blog Index to blocking |
| P3 | S3 | responsive | `/dashboard/agents.html`; `_site/dashboard/agents.html:129` | On a phone the dashboard scrolls sideways; right-hand columns (Merged/Evaluated dates, Top fix) sit off-screen | Playwright at 320px for `/dashboard/agents.html` asserting `scrollWidth <= clientWidth` after data load + BackstopJS mobile scenario; wrap `.data-table` in `overflow-x:auto` div |
| P3 | S4 | responsive | `/blog/` + category pages; `.econ-topic-page`/`.topic-page` vs `.main-content` | Listing/category grids render in a ~852px central column with large side gutters instead of the intended 1040px — feels narrower than the rest of the site | Playwright at 1920px asserting wrapper width ~1040px; add the missing `.main-content:has(.econ-topic-page),:has(.topic-page)` width-unlock override |
| P3 | S3 | consistency | all post pages and category pages; `_layouts/default.html:95` | On mobile, while reading an article or browsing a category, the bottom nav highlights nothing (lost "you are here" cue) | Playwright at 320px navigating to a post and a category URL asserting Blog link has `is-active`/`aria-current=page` (exactly one active); fix `default.html:95` matcher |
| P3 | S4 | consistency | `/blog/`, 3 category pages, `/about/`; include only in `index.md:161`, `post.html:238` | The newsletter signup CTA is absent on the archive, category, and about pages while present on home + every article | Cross-page Playwright spec asserting `aside.newsletter-signup` visible on the intended page-type allowlist (excluding `/search/`,`/404/`); move include into shared layout |
| P3 | S4 | coverage-gap | `/search/` | Nothing today; bespoke chip/result/empty-state UI has no baseline, so a future regression would ship undetected | BackstopJS `/search/` scenario at 3 viewports with onReadyScript clicking a chip; extend `navigation.spec.ts` to assert no chip-row overflow + result within container at 320px |
| P3 | S3 | coverage-gap | `/404/` (`navigation.spec.ts:234-253`) | Nothing guaranteed; the only test self-skips when no hard 404 is returned, and there is no visual baseline for the error page readers hit on dead links | Add BackstopJS `404 Page` scenario at 3 viewports; refactor the test to navigate to `/404/` and assert heading/body/nav unconditionally |
| P3 | S3 | coverage-gap | `_site/YYYY/MM/DD/*/` post template (code/chart blocks) | Nothing today; only 2 of 24 posts are baselined and neither contains code or charts, so those variants are unguarded (note: no tables/blockquotes exist site-wide) | Blocking baselines for the 2 code-block posts and 2 chart posts (or one kitchen-sink fixture) at 3 viewports; fix stale backstop URL `/2026/01/01/`→`/2026/01/02/`; drop `continue-on-error` |

> No P0 defects were confirmed. The S2 coverage-gaps (#13/#14) are the closest to systemic, but their reader impact is latent rather than currently visible, so they sit at P1 rather than P0.

## 3. Gate Gaps (grouped coverage holes + minimal new specs)

**Gap A — No blocking pixel gate exists at all (the meta-gap).**
BackstopJS is the only pixel-diff tool and runs `continue-on-error: true` in `test-quality.yml:142-144` (report upload gated `if: failure()`), the blocking Playwright suite has zero `toHaveScreenshot`/`toMatchSnapshot` assertions, and the `@visual` layout tests were nuclear-healed to accept any positioning. Net: every visual/CSS regression on already-covered pages can land on `main`.
- *Minimal close:* Remove `continue-on-error` from the visual-regression step and make BackstopJS a required check; commit reference bitmaps for the existing 5×3 scenarios; change the report upload to `if: always()`. Replace the vacuous coordinate checks in `responsive.spec.ts` with `gridTemplateColumns` track-count assertions (3/2/1 at 1920/768/320).

**Gap B — Whole page types are never loaded by any gate.**
`backstop.json` has only 5 scenarios (Homepage, Blog Index, 2 posts, About). The three category pages, `/agents/*`, `/dashboard/` + `/dashboard/agents.html`, `/search/`, and `/404/` have no BackstopJS baseline, and Playwright either never visits them or only presence-checks them. This single hole is the direct cause of defects #1, #2, #9, #10, #15, #18, #19.
- *Minimal close:* Add BackstopJS scenarios for `/security/`, `/software-engineering/`, `/test-automation/`, `/search/` (with onReadyScript clicking a chip), and `/404/` at all three viewports. Add one CI invariant that walks every `_site/**/index.html` (or `sitemap.xml` `<loc>`) asserting doctype/`<html>`/site-chrome — this catches layout-less `/agents/*` and any future stray page type in one test.

**Gap C — No viewport-overflow assertion anywhere (320px).**
No spec compares `document.documentElement.scrollWidth` to `clientWidth`; the overflow check at `responsive.spec.ts:723-724` was disabled as "too fragile" and the image test loosened to a 5000px ceiling. Causes #2, #6, and the live `/dashboard/agents.html` overflow.
- *Minimal close:* One Mobile-Chrome (320×568) Playwright spec iterating every page type plus the 4 chart-bearing posts, asserting `scrollWidth <= clientWidth + 1` and reporting the first node whose `getBoundingClientRect().right` exceeds `clientWidth`. Add a 390px pass for the chart posts.

**Gap D — No content/markup-quality assertions on built HTML.**
Specs check structure/role/byline but never excerpt text, `<head>` tag uniqueness, caption casing/length, card-class canonicality, or `<picture>`/WebP usage. Causes #4, #5, #7, #8, #9, #12.
- *Minimal close:* (1) Content-quality spec across `/`, `/blog/`, category pages asserting no teaser starts with `/^Source:/i`; (2) head-metadata uniqueness spec asserting exactly one `title`/canonical/`meta description` per representative page type (no `.first()`); (3) cross-page consistency spec asserting a single canonical post-card selector and `aside.newsletter-signup` on the intended allowlist; (4) an HTML lint failing when any `_site` raw `<img>` `.png` has a `.webp` sibling. Back content rules with `validate-post-quality.sh` extensions (image+`*Source:*` opener; `image_caption` length budget).

**Gap E — Width-vs-design-token never asserted.**
Playwright runs at 320/768/1920 but only checks overflow/visibility, never a container's computed width against its intended `max-width`, so dead `max-width:1040px` ceilings pass. Causes #1 (dimensional part) and #3.
- *Minimal close:* At 1920px, assert `.topic-page`/`.econ-topic-page` boundingBox width ≈ 1040px and first `.topic-card` width ≥ ~280px.

## 4. Verify-next (re-confirm in a real browser)

| # | Defect | URL path | Width | What to confirm |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | Category 3-column grid crushed into ~556px column | `/security/` | 1440px | Cards ≈153px wide, titles wrapping 3–4 lines, ~442px empty gutters each side |
| 2 | High-res PNG chart overflow + horizontal page scroll | `/2026/01/19/the-surprising-economics-of-test-automation-roi/` | 320px | `documentElement.scrollWidth` (~524) > `clientWidth` (320); chart pinned at 500px and clipped right |
| 3 | "Source:" citation leading listing-card excerpts | `/blog/` | 1440px | ~half the `.econ-card-excerpt` cards open with "Source: …" instead of prose |
| 4 | Agent pages render as bare unstyled fragments | `/agents/code-reviewer/` | 1440px | No header/nav/footer, browser-default black-on-white text, no doctype/head chrome |
| 5 | Dashboard data tables overflow on phone | `/dashboard/agents.html` | 320px | Page scrolls sideways after data load; right-hand columns (dates, Top fix) off-screen |
